docs.title: ASML Microsoft Copilot Studio POC
docs.text: Welcome to the ASML Microsoft Copilot Studio POC.

docs.title: Testing🧪 Try the agent
docs.text:
The below steps outline how to test the Copilot Studio agent solution within Microsot Teams.

Once you're signed in Teams, navigate to Apps, located in the left-hand side navigation. Click on it.

If you know the name of the Copilot Studio agent you're about to test, you can make use of the Search option. Otherwise, you can find it under the category Built with Power Platform. Once you have located it, click on it.

In the next step, you'll be prompted by a dialogue box to add this agent to your Teams repository of tools. Click on Add. After a few seconds, you'll be informed that the agent was added successfully. Click Open to start interacting with it.

Interact with your agent.

For convenience, feel free to Pin your agent on the left-hand side navigation, for easy access.

docs.title: Logging👥 Create a new browser profile (Microsoft Edge) - Optional🗝️ Log on to your account
docs.text:
This section contains everything you need for logging in and accessing your Copilot Studio POC environment.

It's always good to have a separate browser profile for your work and for workshops like this. This way you can keep all of your credentials separate and not have to worry about logging out of your personal / work accounts. In the example we will be using Microsoft Edge, instead you can also use any other Browser, just make sure to use a profile where you are not logged in with any other Microsoft Account!

Create new profile

  • Open Microsoft Edge

  • Click on the profile icon (picture) on the top right corner

  • Hover over Other profiles and then select select Set up a new work or school profile

This will then open up a new browser window on your taskbar.

  • Right click the Edge icon on your task bar and select Pin that browser window to your taskbar

  • In the new browser window, select Start without your data

  • Then select Confirm and start browsing.

It may prompt you to configure your new browser theme. If this happens, just select Next and then Finish.

  • Go to copilotstudio.microsoft.com

  • Use your enterprise credentials (ASML username/password) and sign in

  • If you're prompted which region you want to use you can stay in the default Netherlands and just click on Get Started

  • After the loading is done you will most likely load into the ASML (Default) (Upgrade) environment, click here to switch to your environment, within this POC, the name will be: PC-xx-CopilotStudio-Dev for the environment in which you will build your Agent

docs.title: 📦 Solution Export📂 Export your Copilot Studio solution⏭️ For future reference: Importing your unmanaged solution in an environment
docs.text:
This section outlines how to export your solution for reuse or deployment in other environments.

Begin by confirming you are in the Development environment, which contains the most up-to-date version of the solution. Then, select it as shown below.

Click on Export Solution from the top menu.

In the side panel that opens, select Publish again before exporting to ensure all recent changes are included in the solution.

Then, click Next

Althought this might not be the case, during the pre-export validation, some required components may be identified as missing. To ensure the solution functions correctly when imported, it is recommended to add these components by selecting them and clicking Add to solution.

Back to the export wizard, a version number will be suggested, which you can adjust if needed. It is highly important that you select Unmanaged for your solution export as this ensures it remains editable upon import into another environment. Leave the "Run solution checker on export" selected, for a final validation. Then, click Export

The side panel will collapse and a ribbon informing you that the solution is currently being exported will display. Depending on the size and assets contained in the solution, it can take a few minutes until it's exported successfully.

Once the provisioning of your unmanaged solution package is complete, you'll be prompted to download it locally. Click on Download

Your solution is now downloaded and saved as a .zip file to your local machine, typically in the Downloads folder or another location based on your broswer settings.

As a final step, navigate to the designated folder in the Copilot Studio Teams General channel and upload your solution there.

In the future, once your organization provides an environment for importing the solution, ensure you are in the correct environment and select Import Solution from the top menu.

An import dialogue box will be displayed. Click on Browse

Select your unmanaged solution and click Open

After selecting the .zip file, click Next. A validation process will then evaluate the solution.

Lastly, you will be presented with the known details of your solution. Click Import

Once initiated, the side panel will close, and a notification ribbon will display the progress of the import.

Upon completion of the import process, the solution will be available in the selected environment as an unmanaged solution.

docs.title: Training📂 Create a solution✨ Develop an agent🚀 Deploy your agent to testers (approval required)🧑‍🔬 Lead Ambassador: Review request and approve/reject Agent for deployment to test users - this step is NOT for the Developers - Returning to the steps the Developers need to follow🧪 Sharing the Agent with test users who DO NOT HAVE an M365 Copilot license🧪 Sharing the Agent with test users who DO HAVE an M365 Copilot license
docs.text:
Training materials and walkthroughs for the POC. This document will outline how to set up your environment for Agent development and how to use Copilot Studio

Best Practice for everything in the Power Platform (and therefore Copilot Studio): Work INSIDE solutions. They are great for organizing your customizations and some features only work here.

Because of that our first step in the Copilot Studio portal is to navigate to Solutions under the [...]-Button on the left hand side

After the Solutions portal opens, click + New Solution

In the dialog side-bar which opens, give your solution a meaningful name for the usecase and either choose the Default Publisher for orgxx (default) or create your own New Publisher. After entering the details, click Create Note: Using the default publisher is not considered best practice because you have no control about the technical prefix all your components will receive

Your solution is created and currently empty. You can now start building an agent (or any other asset) by opening the Solution, navigating to + New and choose the component type. Once saved it will automatically be added to the provisioned solution.

--- Agent development will be covered by the trainings starting January 8th ---

Once the Agent has been built, tested and published, you can move on to deploy the Agent to the Test environment where you can scope it to your test users for functionality testing and feedback gathering. To move the agent from the Dev-environment to the Test-environment, go back to this Solution window and choose Deploy Pipelines (rocket icon) from the left hand menu

Click Deploy Here to start the process

On the next screen a new version will be automatically assigned to the solution and AI generated deployment notes. Important: It is of upmost importance that you type in the Deployment Notes the e-mail addresses of the users in scope for testing the agent, followed by by whether they have an M365 Copilot license or not. DO NOT SKIP THIS STEP!

All Approvers (sector leads) will be informed via both Teams and Outlook of an initiated deployment. The first to approve/reject will be sufficient to cancel out the rest (first to respond logic).

Once the request is approved/rejected it will be archived

After approval, the solution will land in the Test environment under Managed Solutions, the core-components and configurations will be read-only.

The original maker has access to this managed solution and can view its contents

For the requested test users who DO NOT HAVE an M365 Copilot license, the maker needs to share the agent through Copilot Studio so they can test it in the testing pane (similarly how the developer tests the agent while developing). Do so by making sure you are in the Copilot Studio portal, in the Test environment. Look for your agent in the agent list. Select the agent and click Share

In the dialogue box that opens, type the name of the Test user who does not have an M365 Copilot license. Add it, click on it and select Editor access. Click Update

Instruct the user to go to Copilot Studio in the Test environment, (s)he will find the Agent there and can interact with the agent in the Agent Tester side-pane

For the requested test users who DO HAVE an M365 Copilot license, the maker can share the agent by deploying it to Teams, right after Publishing it. To Publish the agent, open it and click on Publish, located in the upper right corner of the Copilot editor pane.

You will be once again prompted to review any potential issues, warnings, before publishing. Review, then click Publish

You are now ready to expose this agent in Teams so that your M365 Copilot license holder testers can experiment with it. To do so, navigate to the Channels tab, select Teams and Microsoft 365 Copilot, in the dialogue box that opens, unselect the "Make agent available in Microsoft 365 Copilot" and press Add Channel

Next, it is IMPORTANT for the maker to select Edit Details and further describe what the agent does, and instructions for the Test users how to interact and use the agent

Once you Save the changes, you'll be directed a screen back. Otherwise you can also click the Back arrow displayed on the upper left corner of this dialogue box

Once the Teams integration is created and the agent description is added, the maker will be prompted to select who to share it with. Click Availability options and then Show my teammatest and shared users

SIDE NOTE: While you are active in this dialogue box, make sure to also Copy link and save it separately. This is the agent app link which, if shared with the Tester will also direct them to Teams, directly to the Agent for them to add as an App and start testing (it can, at times, prove to accelerate the process and saves Testers the time to look for the agent in the Teams store). This is not a mandatory step, but a remark.

Continuing, after having selecting Show my teammatest and shared users as instructed a step above, in the new pop-up window that appeared, add the testers who do have an M365 Copilot license, they will automatically be granted Viewer permissions, click Update. Viewer permissions allows them to later test this agent directly in Teams.

Depending on your ogranization's processes, your agent might now be submitted to an Teams Administrator to approve its availability in Teams. After the approval from the Teams Admin Center it will take a couple of hourse before the agent becomes available in Teams, from where the Test users can install and start testing it (described in the Testing tab)
